pattern: Sliding the right end while incrementally tracking distinct values of each parity finds the longest window where the two counts match in quadratic time.
hints: A subarray is balanced when its distinct even values number the same as its distinct odd values, so duplicates inside the window count only once.
Fixing the left end and extending the right end lets you maintain two growing sets of distinct values cheaply.
For each left index, walk the right index forward, adding each value to its parity's set, and record the window length whenever the two set sizes are equal.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate even does not change distinct count
best window is in the middle
single element can never be balanced
all odd values, no balanced subarray
repeats inflate length but not distinct counts

stress.description: small arrays over a narrow value range so duplicates and parities mix
stress.note: kept small so the cubic oracle stays fast
